intro: Mesopteris kiauensis is a rare and endemic plant species found in the tropical forests of Borneo, belonging to the family Mesopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and enhancing immune function. The bioactive constituents of Mesopteris kiauensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mesopteris kiauensis include tea, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
